title: Create norn-macros crate and implement core ToolArgs derive
purpose: Establish the norn-macros proc-macro crate as a workspace member and implement the core ToolArgs derive macro that generates json_schema() from struct definitions with String fields. This is the foundation every subsequent macro brief depends on — the crate must compile, the derive must produce valid JSON Schema objects, and the norn crate must re-export the macro for tool authors to use.
task: Create the norn-macros crate with proc-macro = true, add it as a dependency of norn, implement the #[derive(ToolArgs)] proc macro that generates a json_schema() -> serde_json::Value inherent method. This brief covers only String and bool field types — complex types (Option, Vec, enums) are NTM-002. The derive must: extract doc comments as descriptions, build the properties/required/additionalProperties structure, respect field declaration order, and handle #[serde(default)] to omit fields from required. The norn crate re-exports ToolArgs and tool_follow_ups (as a no-op stub re-export until NTM-004 fills it in).

requirements.title: Create norn-macros crate as workspace member
requirements.spec: The norn-macros crate SHALL exist at crates/norn-macros/ with proc-macro = true in Cargo.toml. It SHALL be a workspace member. Cargo.toml SHALL declare [lints] workspace = true. Dependencies SHALL be exactly: proc-macro2, quote, syn (with full and extra-traits features). No other dependencies.
acceptance: crates/norn-macros/Cargo.toml exists with [lib] proc-macro = true
Cargo.toml [dependencies] lists exactly proc-macro2, quote, and syn
syn dependency has features = ["full", "extra-traits"]
Cargo.toml contains [lints] workspace = true
The root Cargo.toml workspace members list includes crates/norn-macros
cargo check -p norn-macros succeeds with zero errors

requirements.title: Add norn-macros as dependency of norn and set up re-exports
requirements.spec: The norn crate SHALL declare norn-macros as a dependency. norn SHALL re-export the ToolArgs derive macro via pub use norn_macros::ToolArgs in the tool module. norn SHALL re-export the tool_follow_ups macro via pub use norn_macros::tool_follow_ups in the tool module. The tool_follow_ups macro SHALL be a no-op placeholder in this brief (expands to nothing) — NTM-004 replaces it with the real implementation.
acceptance: crates/norn/Cargo.toml [dependencies] includes norn-macros = { path = "../norn-macros" }
crates/norn/src/tool/mod.rs contains pub use norn_macros::ToolArgs
crates/norn/src/tool/mod.rs contains pub use norn_macros::tool_follow_ups
use norn::tool::ToolArgs compiles from external crate code
cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors

requirements.title: Implement proc-macro entry point and module structure
requirements.spec: lib.rs SHALL declare the #[proc_macro_derive(ToolArgs, attributes(tool_args))] entry point and a #[proc_macro] tool_follow_ups entry point. lib.rs SHALL delegate to submodules: tool_args/ for derive logic, follow_up/ for the follow-up macro. lib.rs SHALL contain only the proc_macro entry functions and mod declarations — no logic.
acceptance: crates/norn-macros/src/lib.rs declares #[proc_macro_derive(ToolArgs, attributes(tool_args))]
crates/norn-macros/src/lib.rs declares #[proc_macro] pub fn tool_follow_ups
lib.rs contains mod tool_args and mod follow_up declarations
lib.rs contains no struct, enum, or trait definitions
The tool_follow_ups proc macro returns an empty TokenStream (placeholder)

requirements.title: Parse struct fields, doc comments, and basic serde attributes
requirements.spec: The tool_args/parse.rs module SHALL parse a DeriveInput into a structured representation: struct name, fields with name/type/doc-comment/serde-attributes. For each field, it SHALL extract: the field ident, the type path, all /// doc comment lines joined with spaces, and the presence of #[serde(default)] or #[serde(default = "fn")]. Fields with #[serde(skip)] or #[serde(skip_deserializing)] SHALL be marked as skipped.
acceptance: crates/norn-macros/src/tool_args/parse.rs exists with a parse function that accepts syn::DeriveInput
Doc comments (syn::Attribute with path doc) are extracted and joined with spaces
Multiple /// lines produce a single space-joined description string
#[serde(default)] is detected and flags the field as having a default
#[serde(default = "some_fn")] is detected identically to #[serde(default)]
#[serde(skip)] and #[serde(skip_deserializing)] flag the field as skipped

requirements.title: Generate JSON Schema for struct with String and bool fields
requirements.spec: The tool_args/schema.rs module SHALL generate a json_schema() inherent method that returns a serde_json::Value. The generated schema SHALL have type: object, a properties object with one entry per non-skipped field, a required array containing fields that are not #[serde(default)] and not Option<T>, and additionalProperties: false. String fields SHALL generate {"type": "string"}. bool fields SHALL generate {"type": "boolean"}. Property order in the generated JSON SHALL match field declaration order.
acceptance: A struct with fields path: String, content: String generates a schema with properties.path.type == "string" and properties.content.type == "string"
Both fields appear in the required array
The schema includes "additionalProperties": false
A field with #[serde(default)] is absent from the required array
A field with #[serde(skip)] does not appear in properties at all
A bool field generates {"type": "boolean"}
Properties appear in the same order as struct field declarations
Doc comments appear as description values on the corresponding properties

requirements.title: Wire derive orchestration in tool_args/mod.rs
requirements.spec: tool_args/mod.rs SHALL export a derive function that: parses the DeriveInput via parse.rs, builds the schema via schema.rs, and returns the generated impl block as a TokenStream. The generated code SHALL reference ::serde_json::json! and ::serde_json::Value by absolute path. Errors from parsing SHALL use syn::Error::new_spanned to produce compile errors pointing to the offending item.
acceptance: tool_args/mod.rs exports a pub fn derive(input: TokenStream) -> TokenStream
Generated code uses ::serde_json::json! (absolute path, not relative)
Generated code uses ::serde_json::Value in the return type
A struct without fields generates a schema with empty properties and empty required
cargo check -p norn-macros succeeds with zero errors

requirements.title: Integration tests for core derive
requirements.spec: Integration tests SHALL verify the derive macro produces correct schemas for representative structs. Tests live in crates/norn-macros/tests/. Each test applies #[derive(ToolArgs)] to a struct, calls json_schema(), and asserts the schema shape. Tests SHALL cover: all-required fields, mixed required/default fields, skipped fields, doc comments as descriptions, empty struct, and bool fields.
acceptance: crates/norn-macros/tests/tool_args.rs exists with at least 6 test cases
Test: struct with 3 String fields produces schema with all 3 in required and properties
Test: struct with 1 String + 1 #[serde(default)] String has only the first in required
Test: struct with #[serde(skip)] field excludes that field from properties entirely
Test: doc comment text appears verbatim in description
Test: multi-line doc comments are space-joined into one description
Test: empty struct produces {"type": "object", "required": [], "properties": {}, "additionalProperties": false}
All tests pass with cargo test -p norn-macros

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement type mappings beyond String and bool — complex types (integers, floats, Option, Vec, HashMap, Value, nested structs, enums) are NTM-002
SHALL NOT implement serde rename, rename_all, or flatten attribute handling — those are NTM-003
SHALL NOT implement #[tool_args(...)] override attributes — those are NTM-003
SHALL NOT implement compile-time doc comment enforcement (compile_error on missing docs) — that is NTM-003
SHALL NOT implement the real tool_follow_ups macro — only the no-op placeholder; NTM-004 replaces it
SHALL NOT modify any existing tool implementations — migration is NTM-005
verification: cargo check -p norn-macros succeeds with zero errors
cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn-macros -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo test -p norn-macros passes all tests
grep -rn 'use serde' crates/norn-macros/src/ returns no matches (macro crate must not depend on serde)
crates/norn-macros/src/lib.rs contains only proc_macro entry points and mod declarations
crates/norn-macros/src/tool_args/mod.rs contains only pub mod and the derive orchestration function
All generated code references ::serde_json by absolute path
